author: Carlos Rivera
title: B2B Intent Data + AI: The Targeting Stack That's Replacing Spray-and-Pray
description: Intent data alone is noisy. AI alone is generic. Combined correctly, they produce a targeting stack that identifies in-market buyers before your competitors do.

sections.p: Third-party intent data has been available for years. Most B2B teams tried it, got overwhelmed by the volume of low-confidence signals, and went back to lookalike targeting. The problem was never the data — it was the lack of an AI layer to score, prioritize, and act on it intelligently. That layer now exists, and the teams that have wired it up are identifying in-market buyers 30–60 days before their competitors.

sections.h2: How intent signals become buying signals

sections.p: A single intent signal — a company's employees searching for 'enterprise project management software' — is weak. Ten intent signals across 30 days, combined with technographic data (they run Jira), firmographic data (Series B, 200 employees), and first-party signals (visited your pricing page twice), become a high-confidence buying signal.

The AI layer's job is to weight and combine these signals dynamically. Different combinations predict different stages of the buying cycle. The model improves as it sees which signal combinations led to closed deals.

list: Intent data: Bombora or G2 Buyer Intent for third-party signals.
Enrichment: Clay to pull firmographic, technographic, and contact-level data.
Scoring: a simple AI model (even a well-prompted Claude instance with a CSV) to rank accounts by signal strength.
Activation: high-intent accounts trigger personalized outbound sequences; medium-intent accounts go into paid retargeting; low-intent stay in nurture.
Feedback loop: closed-won accounts are fed back into the model to improve scoring accuracy.

sections.p: Most teams underweight their own first-party signals relative to third-party data. Your CRM, product analytics, and website data are higher-fidelity than any vendor's intent network. A prospect who has read 4 blog posts on your site, visited pricing twice, and opened your last 3 emails is telling you something more reliable than a third-party 'intent surge.'
Build the scoring model to heavily weight first-party signals and use third-party data to find net-new accounts showing similar patterns.

The biggest ROI from intent + AI isn't better targeting of accounts that are already evaluating you. It's identifying accounts that are entering an evaluation cycle before they've reached out to anyone. The team that gets in front of a buyer in week 1 of their research shapes how they think about the problem — which is a structural advantage over everyone who shows up in week 6.

sections.quote: Intent data tells you who's looking. AI tells you who's actually going to buy. The difference is 60 days of sales cycle.
